mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions, increasing consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high PAYEMS regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with low rates (FEDFUNDS) signals accommodative policy, creating uncertainty that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high CPIAUCSL/low FEDFUNDS regimes exceed 1.5% or Rank IC > 0.05 in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) above 8% signals economic weakness, reducing corporate profits and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high UNRATE regimes exceed -0.5% or Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weak supply-side activity, reducing corporate earnings and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in low INDPRO regimes exceed 2.0% or Rank IC < 0.04 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) reflect elevated demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high HOUST regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand for housing, increasing construction activity and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high RSAFS/HOUST regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures, prompting tighter monetary policy and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high CPIAUCSL regimes exceed 1.0% or Rank IC > 0.04 over 3-year windows.
